title: Hello World
focus_points: Start with step: main.
Put executable work inside do:.
Use display for immediate output.

title: Set + Display
description: Create values with set, convert them when needed, and display readable output.
focus_points: Variables are introduced with set.
Use added to for text building.
Convert numbers with as text when displaying them in a sentence.
step_source:
step: main
    do:
        set learner to "Ada"
        set finished_lessons to 3
        display "Hello, " added to learner
        display "Lessons finished: " added to (finished_lessons as text)

title: If / Otherwise
description: Branch based on a value and show a different result for each path.
focus_points: Use if for the first condition.
Use otherwise if for extra branches.
End with otherwise for a default path.
step_source:
step: main
    do:
        set score to 85
        if score is greater than or equal to 90
            display "Excellent"
        otherwise if score is greater than or equal to 70
            display "Passing"
        otherwise
            display "Keep practicing"

title: Loop Through a List
description: Build a small list and repeat through each item.
focus_points: Lists use square brackets.
repeat for each walks one item at a time.
Each loop can display or transform the current value.
step_source:
step: main
    do:
        set colors to ["red", "blue", "green"]
        repeat for each color in colors
            display "Color: " added to color

title: Tables + Lookups
description: Store named values in a table and read them back by key.
focus_points: Tables use key/value pairs.
Read a value with table["key"].
Use set table["key"] to add or update values.
step_source:
step: main
    do:
        set person to ["name": "Mina", "age": 12]
        display person["name"]
        set person["city"] to "Boston"
        display person["city"]

title: Simple Riser
description: Use a riser to keep the main step small and readable.
focus_points: Risers are private helpers inside the visible step.
Call a riser with call ... storing result in ... .
Return values make helpers reusable.
step_source:
step: main
    returns: message

    riser: build_message
        returns: result

        do:
            set result to "Hello from a riser"
            return result

    do:
        call build_message storing result in message
        display message
        return message

title: Input Echo
description: Read one line of input and display it back to the learner.
focus_points: Use input to read one line from the Input panel.
Combine text with added to.
This pattern is useful for prompts and mini-programs.
step_source:
step: main
    do:
        display "What is your name?"
        set name to input
        display "Hello, " added to name

title: Countdown Mini-Program
description: A small complete example with a variable, a while loop, and final output.
focus_points: Use repeat while for state-based repetition.
Update the loop variable each pass.
Finish with one final message.
step_source:
step: main
    do:
        set countdown to 3
        repeat while countdown is greater than 0
            display countdown as text
            set countdown to countdown - 1
        display "Go!"
